statement.p: Solve the inequality -2-2(2x+1)\gt4-(3-x)\text{.} Graph the solution set on a number line. State the solution set using both interval notation and set-builder notation.

solution.p: We start by simplifying the two sides.
And now add/subtract terms to separate the variable terms and the constant terms.
And now divide each side by -5\text{.} Since we are dividing by a negative number, the inequality sign will change direction.
Graphically, we represent this solution set as:
Using interval notation, we write the solution set as (-\infty,-1)\text{.} Using set-builder notation, we write it as \{x \mid x \lt -1\}\text{.}
We should check that some number less than -11 is a solution, that -1 itself is not a solution, and that some number greater than -1 is not a solution.
Everything worked out as expected, so our solution is reasonably checked.

webwork-reps.pg:
############################################# ### Generated from PreTeXt source ### ### https://pretextbook.org ### ############################################# ## DBsubject() ## DBchapter() ## DBsection() ## Level() ## KEYWORDS() ## TitleText1() ## EditionText1() ## AuthorText1() ## Section1(not reported) ## Problem1(1.2.16) ## Author() ## Institution() ## Language(en-US)
DOCUMENT();
############################################################ # Load Macros ############################################################ loadMacros( "PGstandard.pl", "PGML.pl", "AnswerFormatHelp.pl", "PGlateximage.pl", "PCCmacros.pl", "contextInequalitySetBuilder.pl", "parserGraphTool.pl", "Pd.pl", "PGcourse.pl", ); COMMENT('Authored in PreTeXt');
############################################################ # Header ############################################################ TEXT(beginproblem());
############################################################ # PG Setup Code ############################################################ Context("Interval"); $interval = Interval("(-inf,-1)"); InequalitySetBuilder::UseVerticalSuchThat(); Context("InequalitySetBuilder-Only"); $setbuilder = Compute("{ x | x<-1}"); $gt = GraphTool("{interval, $interval}")->with( availableTools => ['IntervalTool', 'IncludeExcludePointTool'], numberLine => 1, xAxisLabel => 'x', bBox => [ -8, 8 ], ticksDistanceX => 1, minorTicksX => 0, useBracketEnds => 1, showInStatic => 0, showCoordinateHints => 0, );
$image_1 = createLaTeXImage(); $image_1->addToPreamble(latexImagePreamble()); $image_1->BEGIN_LATEX_IMAGE \begin{tikzpicture} \begin{axis}[numberline] \addplot[infiniteopeninterval] coordinates {(-10,0) (-1,0)}; \end{axis} \end{tikzpicture}
END_LATEX_IMAGE
############################################################ # Body ############################################################
BEGIN_PGML Solve the inequality [`\newcommand{\gt}{>}-2-2(2x+1)\gt4-(3-x)\text{.}`] Graph the solution set on a number line. State the solution set using both interval notation and set-builder notation.
[_]{$gt}{1}
[@KeyboardInstructions('In interval notation.')@]**
[_]{$interval}{12}
[@KeyboardInstructions('In set-builder notation. (If needed, type [|inf|]* or [|infinity|]* for [`\infty\text{,}`] [|>=|]* for [`\geq\text{,}`] and [|<=|]* for [`\leq\text{.}`])')@]**
[_]{$setbuilder}{12}
END_PGML
############################################################ # Solution ############################################################
BEGIN_PGML_SOLUTION We start by simplifying the two sides.
[```\newcommand{\gt}{>}\newcommand{\amp}{&} \begin{aligned} -2-2(2x+1)\amp\gt4-(3-x)\\ -2-4x-2\amp\gt4-3+x\\ -4x-4\amp\gt1+x \end{aligned} ```]
And now add/subtract terms to separate the variable terms and the constant terms.
[```\newcommand{\addright}[1]{{\highlight{{{}+#1}}}}\newcommand{\subtractright}[1]{{\highlight{{{}-#1}}}}\newcommand{\gt}{>}\newcommand{\amp}{&} \begin{aligned} -4x-4\addright{4}\subtractright{x}\amp\gt1+x\addright{4}\subtractright{x}\\ -5x\amp\gt5 \end{aligned} ```]
And now divide each side by [`-5\text{.}`] Since we are dividing by a negative number, the inequality sign will change direction.
[```\newcommand{\lt}{<}x\lt-1```]
Graphically, we represent this solution set as:
>> [@image(insertGraph($image_1), width=>600)@]* <<
Using interval notation, we write the solution set as [`(-\infty,-1)\text{.}`] Using set-builder notation, we write it as [`\newcommand{\lt}{<}\{x \mid x \lt -1\}\text{.}`]
We should check that some number less than [`-11`] _is_ a solution, that [`-1`] itself is _not_ a solution, and that some number greater than [`-1`] is _not_ a solution.
[```\newcommand{\substitute}[1]{{\color{blue}{{#1}}}}\newcommand{\confirm}[1]{\stackrel{\checkmark}{#1}}\newcommand{\wonder}[1]{\stackrel{\text{?}}{#1}}\newcommand{\gt}{>}\newcommand{\amp}{&} \begin{aligned} -2-2(2(\substitute{-2})+1)\amp\wonder{\gt}4-(3-(\substitute{-2}))\\ -2-2(-4+1)\amp\wonder{\gt}4-5\\ -2-2(-3)\amp\wonder{\gt}-1\\ -2+6\amp\wonder{\gt}-1\\ 4\amp\confirm{\gt}-1 \end{aligned} ```]
[```\newcommand{\substitute}[1]{{\color{blue}{{#1}}}}\newcommand{\reject}[1]{\stackrel{\text{no}}{#1}}\newcommand{\wonder}[1]{\stackrel{\text{?}}{#1}}\newcommand{\gt}{>}\newcommand{\amp}{&} \begin{aligned} -2-2(2(\substitute{-1})+1)\amp\wonder{\gt}4-(3-(\substitute{-1})) \amp -2-2(2(\substitute{0})+1)\amp\wonder{\gt}4-(3-(\substitute{0}))\\ -2-2(-2+1)\amp\wonder{\gt}4-4 \amp -2-2(0+1)\amp\wonder{\gt}4-3\\ -2-2(-1)\amp\wonder{\gt}0 \amp -2-2(1)\amp\wonder{\gt}1\\ -2+2\amp\wonder{\gt}0 \amp -2-2\amp\wonder{\gt}1\\ 0\amp\reject{\gt}0 \amp -4\amp\reject{\gt}1 \end{aligned} ```]
Everything worked out as expected, so our solution is reasonably checked.
END_PGML_SOLUTION
############################################################ # End Problem ############################################################
ENDDOCUMENT();
